_comment: Connector-specific edits to GENERATED files that a cli-printing-press reprint must NOT clobber. check_handfixes.py asserts every marker below is still present (and banned anti-patterns absent) and fails CI if a reprint dropped one.

handfixes.summary: Authorization header must be `API-Key <token>` (spec x-auth-value-prefix); AuthHeader() prepends the prefix and is idempotent
handfixes.why: PandaDoc authenticates with `Authorization: API-Key <key>`, not a bare bearer token. The generator ignores `x-auth-value-prefix` (2nd confirmed instance after sentinelone), so a stock AuthHeader() returns the bare token -> silent live 401. The prior 4.19 print shipped broken (never live-verified). Hand-fixed in config.go (prefix + HasPrefix idempotency) and pinned by pandadoc_auth_test.go. Grep x-auth-value-prefix in spec + AuthHeader in config.go on every reprint.
handfixes.spec_encode_followup: Make the press honor x-auth-value-prefix when emitting AuthHeader(); until then this hand-fix recurs on every reprint.

handfixes.summary: webhook_coverage novel reads the hyphen resource name `webhook-subscriptions` (matches the syncer) with an underscore legacy fallback
handfixes.why: The syncer persists rows under `webhook-subscriptions` (hyphen) but the prior 4.19 novel read `webhook_subscriptions` (underscore), so the coverage report always saw 0 rows. When porting prior novels, every db.List("...") must match defaultSyncResources()/sync.go's resource names. The fix reads the canonical hyphen name first and falls back to the underscore form for legacy local stores.
